hypothesis: If the lattice design–property tensor is sampled sparsely (uniformly or with experimentalist-biased non-uniformity), then tensor-completion surrogates (CPD-S/NeAT and their ensemble) will achieve higher R² in predicting Young’s modulus and mass-normalized modulus than Gaussian processes, XGBoost, and MLPs at the same training size—especially under biased sampling—because they exploit the data’s low-rank structure across design modes.
